steps.title: Scale the object count
steps.content: The total mass of $r$ identical objects is $t$. The new group has $138r$ objects, which is $138$ times as many objects.

steps.title: Scale the mass the same way
steps.content: For identical objects, total mass is proportional to the number of objects. If the object count is multiplied by $138$, the total mass is also multiplied by $138$: $$138\cdot t=138t$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• $138-t$ and $138+t$ subtract or add $138$, but mass must scale by multiplication.
  • Choice C — $\frac{t}{138}$ would make the mass smaller even though there are more objects.
  • Choice D — $138t$ multiplies the original mass by $138$. Correct.
That matches Choice D — $138t$.
